basics.name: Nitish Naineni
basics.summary: Exploring the crossroads of machine learning and software engineering. With a passion for computer vision, deep learning, and real-world applications. I am always looking for new challenges and opportunities to learn and grow.

work.name: University At Buffalo, Buffalo, NY
highlights: Reviewed deep learning and ensemble methods to analyze adversarial attack impacts on ensembles.
Trained ensemble deep learning models using strategies like TRADES, SCORE, MART and assessed them
Improved ensemble diversity by integrating GAL, ADP, DVERGE, and TRS methods into training.
Implemented efficient training process for deep ensembles using vectorized computation and mixed precision.
Improved ensemble model's adversarial accuracy, outperforming single adversarial-trained models.
Evaluated and validated the methodology, proving its effectiveness and theory.

work.name: UST global, Trivandrum, India
highlights: Developed a patent landscaping system using machine learning and IPC standards to find similar published patents.
Improved performance through hierarchical database implementation in MySQL.
Developed a real-time retail analyzer with Cassandra & AWS for inventory tracking and auto-replenishment
Developed a parking allotment system utilizing OCR and image processing for number plate recognition and parking spot allocation. Successfully tested and validated at multiple locations.
Created machine learning models for analytics, customer segmentation, and anomaly detection.
Processed and analyzed large datasets using Python, SQL, and Spark.
Conducted experiments to evaluate machine learning model performance

work.name: Capiot Software, Hyderabad, India
highlights: Developed and tested a Product classification system using hierarchical classification with Word2Vec for vectorization. It accurately assigns UNSPSC codes based on product names and descriptions.
Built and maintained ML Infrastructure and pipelines, enhancing efficiency in model training and deployment.
Expanded ML Platform and frameworks to support scalable model training and deployment.
Collaborated with business and engineering teams to integrate and leverage model outputs.
Enhanced performance and reduced downtime using MLOps with Databricks in the Cloud.
Developed and managed Python scripts for data analysis, model building, and deployment.
Utilized Spark and SQL for processing large datasets in model training

education.institution: Birla institute of technology and science, Hyderabad, India

projects.name: Uncertainty estimation using deep ensembles
projects.summary: This project uses deep ensembles, akin to Bayesian neural networks, to better quantify prediction uncertainties in Deep Learning. By training an ensemble of models to predict varying results for the same input, the variance across these predictions measures the uncertainty. High variance, especially for adversarial samples, indicates greater uncertainty.
highlights: Bayesian neural networks
Pytorch

projects.name: Monocular depth estimation
projects.summary: Estimating depth of a scene with monocular vision using transfer learning. The model uses pretrained densenet as an encoder and a decoder to extract depth from the encoded data. This results in a comparable depth estimation to binocular vision.
highlights: Python
Computer Vision
Deep learning

projects.name: Multi agent flocking
projects.summary: The project deals with a swarm emergent learning system. An approach with multiple autonomous agents which can move in a continuous space by avoiding being caught by a single or multiple predators. The agents use DDPG with awareness of surrounding allies and predators to develop a flocking behavior to avoid the predators.
highlights: Python
Reinforcement learning
Pytorch
PettingZoo

projects.name: Panorama stitching using gradient matching
projects.summary: Utilized ORB feature descriptors to stitch multiple images, employing Depth First Search for matching and propagation. Gradient matching was applied to remove differing foreground moving objects in overlapping areas, ensuring preservation of similar gradient image segments.
highlights: Python
OpenCV
NumPy
matplotlib
